title: malcolmia lacera subsp. broussonetii
intro: Malcolmia lacera subsp. broussonetii, also known as Broussonet's whitlowgrass or Malcolmia broussonetii, is a plant species in the Brassicaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and providing antioxidant effects. Malcolmia lacera subsp. broussonetii can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, although the efficacy and safety of these preparations have not been extensively scientifically studied.
